steps.title: Use Lorde's claim
steps.content: Text 2 argues that professional poetry requires time that full-time workers often lack. Text 1 presents Lorde's opposite emphasis: poets can write in brief moments, including "between shifts" and on the subway.

steps.title: Choose the likely response
steps.content: Choice A brings in earning a living from journals, which Lorde does not discuss. Choice C invents full-time work as a way to pay for training. Choice D shifts to financially rewarding art forms, which is not Lorde's point. The remaining choice directly counters Text 2 with Lorde's view that people can practice poetry while working. The answer is B.
